{{Quote|It's a simple run. Stash a couple hundred bottles of R'alla mineral water in the Falcon's floor compartments, then smuggle the contraband to the clean-living people of Rampa II.|Han Solo, ''Smuggler's Guide''|<ref name="Smuggler's Guide" />}}
'''Rampa II''', also known as '''Rampa''', was an astronomical object in the [[Corporate Sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories]], connected to [[Panna]] in the [[Cronese Mandate]] by the smugglers' [[Hyperspace route|hyperlane]] [[Bubble Wink]]. The former [[pirate]] [[Queen]] [[Maz Kanata]] marked it as a point of interest on a map she included in the [[book]] that would become known as the ''[[Smuggler's Guide]]''.<ref name="Smuggler's Guide" />

{{Planet-stub}}

==History==
Between [[10 BBY]] and [[3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">The entry in ''[[Star Wars: Smuggler's Guide]]'' that mentions 373-CR takes place after [[Han Solo]] has completed the [[Kessel Run]], but before he encounters a [[bounty hunter]] on [[Ord Mantell]], which is mentioned as having occurred prior to the [[Battle of Hoth]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. ''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide]]'' establishes that Solo completes the [[Kessel]] Run nine [[Standard year|years]] after the [[Proclamation of the New Order]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[19 BBY]], meaning Solo's Kessel Run must take place in [[10 BBY]]. The ''Atlas'' also dates the Battle of [[Hoth]] to [[3 ABY]]; therefore, the entry in the [[in-universe]] [[book]] ''[[Smuggler's Guide]]'' must have been written between 10 BBY and 3 ABY.</ref> [[Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca]] worked with the [[Briil Twins]] to smuggle hundred bottles of [[R'alla mineral water]] to the people of Rampa II. Solo hoped that they'll earn enough to get them off the [[Red List]] and shake the debt collectors.<ref name="Smuggler's Guide" />

Later, the pair were intercepted on the [[Rampa Rapids/Canon|Rampa Rapids]] and imprisoned on Rampa II. Then the [[smuggler]] [[Sana Starros]], who wished to claim the belongings of her [[Marriage|husband]] [[Han Solo]] and his co-[[pilot]] [[Chewbacca]], bribed [[Customs official|customs agent]] of the [[Rampa II Port Authority]] [[373-CR]], who approved Starros's merchandise claim form, allowing her to take the gear, which included the [[book]] known as the ''[[Smuggler's Guide]]'', in which Starros wrote about bribing the agent.<ref name="Smuggler's Guide" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rampa II was first mentioned in the [[2018]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Smuggler's Guide]]'', which was written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Smuggler's Guide" /> The Smuggler's Guide entry in which the world is mentioned primarily recounted the events depicted in the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] novel ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="hsr">''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''</ref>
